name: Pickled Cabbage
recipeInstructions:
Cabbage is a frequent constituent of pickles, but this is the first time we have had it pickled alone. This makes a very sharp pickle - almost a condiment that might be used in place of horseradish. It is very good in an oyster cocktail sauce and may be used sparingly as a relish for meats.
1. Slice or shred the cabbage very finely.
2. Place the cabbage in an earthen dish and sprinkle with salt, mixing well.
3. Cover with another dish and let stand for 24 hours.
4. Drain the cabbage and mix enough white wine vinegar to cover it.
5. Add four whole cloves, two blades of mace, and four whole allspice for each pint of cabbage.
6. Boil the vinegar mixture for two minutes and pour over the cabbage.
7. Seal in pint jars.
This amount makes about three pints.
